answer: The purpose of the 'Hello World' tutorial on GitHub is to teach you GitHub essentials like repositories, branches, commits, and pull requests, and to help you create your own Hello World repository and learn GitHub's pull request workflow.

You can think of a repository as a folder that contains related items, such as files, images, videos, or even other folders. A repository usually groups together items that belong to the same "project" or thing you're working on.
A README file contains information about your project and is written in Markdown, which is an easy-to-read, easy-to-write language for formatting plain text.

Commits are saved changes to the files in your repository. Each commit has an associated commit message, which is a description explaining why a particular change was made. Commit messages capture the history of your changes so that other contributors can understand what you’ve done and why.
The changes on your branch will be incorporated into the main branch.
